title: update since my intro post (Feb 3)A few months ago I introdun meshcore-gui

messages.author: pe1hvh
messages.text: A few months ago I introduced meshcore-gui here — a desktop GUI for MeshCore over BLE/USB.. Since then, quite a bit has been added: BBS support — read and post bulletin board messages directly from the desktop BOT support — automate responses and handle mesh commands Multi-device support — run the GUI across multiple nodes simultaneously And for those running multiple independent MeshCore networks, I also published a companion tool: meshcore-bridge — selectively relays messages between channels across separate MeshCore networks. https://github.com/pe1hvh/meshcore-bridge Main repo is still at: https://github.com/pe1hvh/meshcore-gui Still MIT, still Python, feedback and contributions very welcome! 73 de PE1HVH
messages.content_html: A few months ago I introduced meshcore-gui here — a desktop GUI for MeshCore over BLE/USB.. Since then, quite a bit has been added: BBS support — read and post bulletin board messages directly from the desktop BOT support — automate responses and handle mesh commands Multi-device support — run the GUI across multiple nodes simultaneously And for those running multiple independent MeshCore networks, I also published a companion tool: meshcore-bridge — selectively relays messages between channels across separate MeshCore networks. https://github.com/pe1hvh/meshcore-bridge Main repo is still at: https://github.com/pe1hvh/meshcore-gui Still MIT, still Python, feedback and contributions very welcome! 73 de PE1HVH
